points.text: SEED Therapeutics — ST-01156 — Neuroblastoma — Clinical-stage RBM39 molecular glue degrader achieved tumor eradication in an in vivo model; presented at AACR 2026.
Monte Rosa Therapeutics — MRT-8102 — Elevated CVD-risk — NEK7-directed molecular glue degrader demonstrated profound hsCRP reductions in Phase 1; GFORCE-1 trial readout expected H2 2026.

Monte Rosa Therapeutics — MRT-2359 — Metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer — Molecular glue degrader in combination with enzalutamide showed 100% PSA response rate in patients with AR mutations.
Monte Rosa Therapeutics — MRT-6160 — Immune-mediated diseases — VAV1-directed molecular glue degrader advancing toward Novartis-led Phase 2 study initiation.
Monte Rosa Therapeutics — Corporate — Molecular glue platform — Secured $345M upsized financing to fund operations into 2029 and support multiple Phase 2 initiations.
SEED Therapeutics — ST-01156 — Neuroblastoma — Clinical-stage RBM39 molecular glue degrader achieved complete tumor eradication in in vivo models; presented at 2026 AACR Annual Meeting.

Monte Rosa Therapeutics (Nasdaq: GLUE) is a clinical-stage biotechnology company developing novel molecular glue degrader (MGD)-based medicines, reporting positive interim Phase 1 data for its NEK7-directed MGD MRT-8102.
Monte Rosa Therapeutics secured a $345 million upsized follow-on financing to support operations into 2029 and fund Phase 2 study initiations for its molecular glue degrader pipeline.
Monte Rosa Therapeutics presented preclinical data at the AACR 2026 Annual Meeting regarding its Cyclin E1 (CCNE1)-directed molecular glue degrader for treating CCNE1-amplified solid tumors.
SEED Therapeutics — ST-01156 — Neuroblastoma — Clinical-stage RBM39 molecular glue degrader demonstrated tumor eradication in vivo; poster presented at AACR 2026.
Monte Rosa Therapeutics — MRT-8102 — Elevated CVD-risk — NEK7-directed molecular glue degrader Phase 1 data showed profound hsCRP suppression; GFORCE-1 trial readout anticipated H2 2026.

Monte Rosa Therapeutics — MRT-2359 — Metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer — Molecular glue degrader plus enzalutamide achieved 100% PSA response in AR-mutated patients; Phase 2 with apalutamide planned Q3 2026.
Monte Rosa Therapeutics — MRT-6160 — Immune-mediated diseases — VAV1-directed molecular glue degrader advancing toward Novartis-led Phase 2 initiation.
SEED Therapeutics — ST-01156 — Neuroblastoma — Clinical-stage RBM39 molecular glue degrader demonstrated tumor eradication in vivo; poster presentation (LB355) at 2026 AACR Annual Meeting.

Monte Rosa Therapeutics — MRT-8102 — Elevated CVD-risk / Gout / Hidradenitis Suppurativa — NEK7-directed molecular glue degrader; Phase 1 interim data showed profound hsCRP reductions; Phase 2 studies planned for H2 2026 (CVD), Q4 2026/Q1 2027 (gout), and H1 2027 (hidradenitis suppurativa).

Monte Rosa Therapeutics — MRT-2359 — Metastatic Castration-Resistant Prostate Cancer (mCRPC) — VAV1-directed molecular glue degrader; Phase 1/2 combination with enzalutamide showed 100% PSA response rate in patients with AR mutations; Phase 2 with apalutamide anticipated Q3 2026.
Monte Rosa Therapeutics — MRT-6160 — Immune-mediated diseases — VAV1-directed molecular glue degrader; advancing toward anticipated Phase 2 study initiation by Novartis.
Monte Rosa Therapeutics — CCNE1-directed MGD — CCNE1-amplified solid tumors — Preclinical data presented at AACR 2026 Annual Meeting demonstrating potential for tumor treatment.
SEED Therapeutics — Molecular glue degrader development — ST-01156 (RBM39) + 5 other programs — Clinical-stage; RITE3™ platform; backed by Eli Lilly and Eisai.
Monte Rosa Therapeutics — Molecular glue degrader commercialization — MRT-8102, MRT-2359, MRT-6160 — $345M financing secured; cash runway to 2029; multiple Phase 2 initiations planned.

SEED Therapeutics — Rationally designed molecular glue degrader development — ST-01156 (RBM39 degrader) + 5 other programs — Clinical-stage biotech; co-founded by Nobel Laureate Avram Hershko and molecular glue pioneer Michele Pagano; backed by Eli Lilly and Eisai.

Monte Rosa Therapeutics — Molecular glue degrader (MGD) development — MRT-8102, MRT-2359, MRT-6160, CCNE1-MGD — $345M financing secured; cash runway extends to 2029; multiple Phase 2 initiations planned for 2026–2027.

ST-01156 — Mechanism: RBM39 molecular glue degrader — Phase: Clinical — Population: Neuroblastoma (in vivo model) — Endpoints/Results: Tumor eradication; MYC and CDKN2A/B identified as potential response biomarkers.

MRT-8102 — Mechanism: NEK7-directed molecular glue degrader — Phase: 1 (GFORCE-1) — Population: Elevated CVD-risk subjects — Endpoints/Results: Profound hsCRP reductions comparable to biologics; favorable safety/tolerability profile confirmed.

MRT-2359 — Mechanism: Molecular glue degrader (AR pathway) — Phase: 1/2 — Population: Metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC) with AR mutations — Endpoints/Results: 100% PSA response rate in combination with enzalutamide.

MRT-6160 — Mechanism: VAV1-directed molecular glue degrader — Phase: Preclinical/Phase 2 prep — Population: Immune-mediated diseases — Endpoints/Results: Advancing toward Novartis-led Phase 2 initiation.

ST-01156 — Mechanism: RBM39 molecular glue degrader inducing targeted protein degradation via ubiquitin-proteasome system — Phase: Clinical — Population: Neuroblastoma (in vivo model) — Comparator: Not specified — Endpoints/Results: Complete tumor eradication; MYC and CDKN2A/B identified as potential response biomarkers.

MRT-8102 — Mechanism: NEK7-directed molecular glue degrader suppressing NLRP3/IL-1/IL-6 pathway — Phase: 1 (interim) — Population: Subjects with elevated CVD-risk — Comparator: Historical biologic therapy data — Results: Profound hsCRP reductions comparable to or better than biologics; favorable safety/tolerability profile in SAD/MAD cohorts.

MRT-2359 — Mechanism: VAV1-directed molecular glue degrader — Phase: 1/2 — Population: Metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC) patients with AR mutations — Comparator: Enzalutamide combination — Results: 100% PSA response rate observed.

CCNE1-directed MGD — Mechanism: Cyclin E1-directed molecular glue degrader — Phase: Preclinical — Population: CCNE1-amplified solid tumors — Comparator: Not specified — Results: Preclinical data presented at AACR 2026 Annual Meeting.
Monte Rosa Therapeutics — Capital raise — Molecular glue pipeline — Secured $345M upsized follow-on financing to support operations into 2029 and multiple Phase 2 initiations.
Monte Rosa Therapeutics / Novartis — Partnership — MRT-6160 (VAV1-directed MGD) — Novartis to initiate multiple Phase 2 studies in immune-mediated diseases.

Monte Rosa Therapeutics — $345 million upsized follow-on financing — Molecular glue degrader pipeline — Proceeds support operations into 2029 and fund Phase 2 study initiations and clinical data readouts.
Monte Rosa Therapeutics / Novartis — Partnership for MRT-6160 — VAV1-directed molecular glue degrader in immune-mediated diseases — Novartis anticipated to initiate multiple Phase 2 studies.
